content: Principal component analysis can be integrated into the intrinsic curiosity module (ICM) of 2025 autonomous agents to compress high-dimensional environmental state representations before calculating prediction error for intrinsic reward, filtering out noisy task-irreleasing variation that would otherwise generate misleading "novelty" rewards from uninformative random sensory fluctuation. This fixes a longstanding flaw of ICM where agents get stuck exploring noisy sensory distractors instead of task-relevant novel states.
